Credit Card Fraud — GPU kNN Graph + Louvain Community Detection

Kaggle Credit Card Fraud (ULB) · 284,807 transactions · pipeline: cuDF → cuML kNN → cuGraph Louvain → cuML UMAP, all on GPU (RTX 4060, WSL2)

0.961
Louvain modularity
139
communities found
77%
of all fraud in 2 communities
<15s
total GPU compute time

A 15-nearest-neighbor similarity graph was built over the V1–V28 PCA features (no Class label used), then partitioned with Louvain community detection. Two communities — out of 139 — turned out to be overwhelmingly fraud:

CommunitySizeFraudsFraud rate
8132427384.3%
12513410779.9%
all other 137 communities284,3491120.04%

Community assignment derived purely from feature-space similarity (unsupervised) — the Class label was only used afterward to score the communities.

UMAP projection — full range

All 284,807 points; gray = normal, red = fraud. A handful of far-flung outliers stretch the axes.

UMAP projection — zoomed to dense core

Same embedding, cropped to the 99.4% density region. A distinct fraud-only arc separates from the main mass at the right; the rest of the fraud points sit scattered inside the bulk — consistent with the two dominant Louvain communities plus a long tail of smaller ones.

Wait — where's the pattern?

The two plots above color all fraud the same red, including the ~110 fraud cases scattered thinly across dozens of small, mostly-normal communities. That mixes in a lot of visual noise. Isolating just the two dominant fraud communities (88 and 118) and leaving everything else gray tells a clearer story:

Both communities sit in a thin peninsula clearly detached from the main mass — tight, isolated, and almost entirely fraud. The rest of the fraud cases (the ~110 not in either group) are the ones still buried inside the bulk in the earlier plots.
